pr_description: Adds signature verification for incoming webhooks from our payment processor. The processor signs each payload with our shared secret using HMAC-SHA256. We verify the signature before processing the event to prevent spoofed webhooks.
diff:
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/webhooks/verify.py
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+import hashlib
+import hmac
+import time
+from flask import request, abort
+
+WEBHOOK_SECRET = b""  # populated from env at startup
+TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE = 300  # 5 minutes
+
+
+def _compute_expected(payload: bytes, timestamp: str) -> str:
+    """Compute expected HMAC-SHA256 signature."""
+    message = f"{timestamp}.{payload.decode()}".encode()
+    return hmac.new(WEBHOOK_SECRET, message, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
+
+
+def verify_signature(payload: bytes, sig_header: str, timestamp_header: str) -> bool:
+    """Return True if the webhook signature is valid."""
+    try:
+        timestamp = int(timestamp_header)
+    except (ValueError, TypeError):
+        return False
+
+    # Replay attack prevention
+    now = int(time.time())
+    if abs(now - timestamp) > TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE:
+        return False
+
+    expected = _compute_expected(payload, timestamp_header)
+
+    # BUG: timing attack — string == comparison leaks timing information
+    # An attacker can measure response time to guess the signature byte-by-byte
+    # Should use hmac.compare_digest() for constant-time comparison
+    return sig_header == expected
+
+
+def webhook_handler():
+    """Flask view: process a payment processor webhook."""
+    payload = request.get_data()
+    sig = request.headers.get("X-Signature", "")
+    ts = request.headers.get("X-Timestamp", "")
+
+    if not verify_signature(payload, sig, ts):
+        abort(403)
+
+    event = request.get_json(force=True)
+    process_event(event)
+    return "", 200
+
+
+--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/webhooks/token_auth.py
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+import secrets
+from db.models import ApiToken
+
+
+def validate_api_token(provided: str) -> bool:
+    """Check if the provided API token belongs to an active account."""
+    token = ApiToken.query.filter_by(token=provided, active=True).first()
+    # BUG: DB timing reveals whether a token prefix exists even before comparison
+    # Also: comparing ORM objects falls back to == on the token string
+    if token is None:
+        return False
+    # BUG: timing attack — should use secrets.compare_digest, not ==
+    return token.token == provided
+
+
+def generate_api_token() -> str:
+    """Generate a cryptographically secure random API token."""
+    # BUG: 16 bytes = 128 bits; acceptable but 32 bytes preferred for API tokens
+    # More importantly: token is stored as plaintext in DB — should store hash
+    raw = secrets.token_hex(16)
+    return raw  # returned to user once; stored plaintext in ApiToken table
